title: Portfolio Case Studies and Technical Decision Narratives · Career Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs portfolio case studies and technical decision narratives from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for evidence-linked project case-study set after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the affected people, decision, evidence, constraints, prior alternative, and personal authority without inventing user research.
Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Case Study Access
steps.instruction:
review case — A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative.

Inspect the Case Study Access trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Case Study Access.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Case Study Access, keep Case Study Access connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Case Study Access, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Case Study Access, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Case Study Access precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Case Study Access, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. Competency probe: Provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative.

stimulus.title: Case Study Access case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Portfolio accessibility matters only for design roles.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative. Artifact under review: evidence-linked project case-study set.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Case Study Access, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Case Study Access record says evidence-linked project case-study set looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Case Study Access.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Case Study Access without its stated preconditions, omit the evidence-linked project case-study set trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Case Study Access for evidence-linked project case-study set.
